pwrMsc.school: Wrocław University of Science and Technology

highlights: Developed and maintained web services in the Volvo IT environment.
Worked with the .NET stack (C#, ASP) and data integration (SSIS).
Supported work with MS SQL Server and task automation through VB scripts.
Developed web services in the Volvo IT environment.
Worked with .NET technologies (C#, ASP) and MS SQL Server.
Implemented backend services supporting Charging Point Operator (CPO) operations.
Implemented a proxy enabling universal access to data while hiding the complexity of BOCPP, OCPI, and OCPP protocols.
Rebuilt the API layer (GraphQL Federation, gRPC, REST), enabling more flexible and faster access to data.
Supported the migration of responsibilities and data to a Single Source of Truth model, reducing the risk of inconsistency in the system.
outcomes: Simplified access to the entire platform through a consistent API layer independent of the data source.
Enabled real-time data access for services and integrations.
Initiated the use of Golang in the organization, supporting its adoption and the development of team capabilities.

highlights: Creating our own products and services.
Gaining experience through practice and experimentation.
outcomes: Created and launched the AR Flashbulb platform supporting the sales process.
Designed and launched Elepantry — a platform integrating multiple cloud service providers in one place.
Led the company to a sale, gaining hands-on experience in building products from concept to commercialization.

highlights: Developed a payments platform supporting the UK BACS and Faster Payments systems.
Designed and implemented failure recovery mechanisms for transaction processing flows.
Developed solutions supporting system monitoring and observability.
Created CLI tools supporting the work of development and operations teams.
outcomes: Increased the platform's resilience to failures, supporting continuous payment processing.
Improved communication between microservices, increasing the predictability of transaction flows.
Contributed to the adoption of Kubernetes and Cilium, supporting the development of the platform's modern infrastructure.
Made teams' work easier by automating repetitive development and operations tasks.

highlights: Designed and built from scratch a central gaming platform that served as the foundation for multiple games.
Introduced a video streaming system for hosts before the game started (HLS).
Developed mechanisms for fraud detection, game room management, and player matchmaking.
Worked on game server scalability in a P2P architecture.
outcomes: Delivered a scalable platform enabling fast creation and launch of subsequent games.
Enabled the creation of new game formats based on an entertainment model with a live host.
Accelerated the development of new features and product experiments through shared platform components.
Optimized infrastructure usage, supporting efficient platform growth.

highlights: Delivered software solutions for clients using JVM, Go, and cloud technologies (AWS, Azure).
Designed and developed distributed systems based on microservices, asynchronous communication, and cloud-native architecture.
Automated software delivery processes through CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code, and automation tools.
outcomes: Designed and implemented one of the first serverless systems in the organization, using an early cloud-native approach to monitor elevator operations and detect situations requiring intervention.
Delivered a system for managing parcel flow and communication in a logistics environment.

highlights: Led the RDS team (14 people: Wrocław, London, Bangalore).
Managed the backlog, business requirements, and communication with management.
Was responsible for technical design, code review, and team capability development.
Maintained continuity of a critical ETL process supporting VaR calculation and bank risk-related decisions.
outcomes: Gained insight into working in a global financial organization and the requirements imposed on risk management systems.

recGlobal.role: Solutions Architect · Technical Leader · Scrum Master
highlights: Translated clients' business needs into technical solutions, estimates, and system architecture.
Combined solution design with estimation, planning, and project delivery in a fixed-price model.
Built and grew development teams, supporting them in making technical decisions.
outcomes: Reduced the risk of delivering fixed-price projects through more accurate estimation, scope planning, and early problem identification.
Led migrations of systems from legacy technologies to a modern stack.
Received positive feedback and recognition from clients for contributions to project delivery.

highlights: Developed a Learning Management System (J2EE/Spring) — from requirements and issue analysis through to production deployments.
Was responsible for data migrations and safe deployment of changes to production environments.
outcomes: Improved data consistency in the LMS by streamlining migration processes, change handling, and unifying business logic.
Reduced the number of user-reported issues by around 30% by improving system stability and quality.

highlights: Was responsible for subscriber migration between operator network clusters.
Designed and built FatMT — an ETL tool automating subscriber data migration.
Developed BTS modules (Performance Management, Climate Control) in C/C++.
outcomes: Reduced the migration process time from about 2500 hours to about 200 hours through automation and data validation.
Created a reusable solution that could be adapted to the needs of different operators (including Claro, Comcel, Zain, and UMC).
Reduced the risk of errors during migration through automatic validation, reporting, and data correctness control.
